mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity cash flows, directly compressing forward S&P 500 valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate crossing above 5.0 percent signals deteriorating labor market conditions that suppress corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or the threshold effect vanishes in out-of-sample walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that sustains corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in quarterly return regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply chain efficiency, which boosts short-term equity momentum over a one-week horizon during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration yields negative or flat 5-day forward returns during expansion regimes, or the signal reverses direction when GDP growth exceeds 3.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with higher policy rates triggers real rate compression and Fed tightening expectations, which jointly suppress equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect disappears when CPIAUCSL growth remains below 2.0 percent year-over-year, or forward returns remain positive in >55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Steady monthly payroll employment growth provides a reliable leading indicator of consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no statistically significant correlation with quarterly forward returns, or Rank IC falls below 0.01 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that supports earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5 percent coincides with negative forward returns in >50% of observations, or the threshold effect is not robust to alternative growth definitions.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment signals a healthy supply-demand balance that fuels short-term equity momentum over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict positive 5-day returns when either series moves independently, or the interaction term loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.
